instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Things To Do After Buying Term Insurance
Buying term life insurance is a significant step you can take to ensure the well-being of your family. But does your responsibility to protect your family end there? Making the purchase may be the end of the process of getting a life cover, but it is also the first step in ensuring that your plan covers you well and your family gets the protection you want for them.
Any person associated with the policy or benefiting from it should be aware of all the things to do after buying term insurance. After you purchase a term plan, don’t make the mistake of assuming that all that would be left now, would be the payment of premiums. You should take some steps to ensure you get the most out of the policy and your duration as a policyholder.
Steps After Getting a Term Plan
Here are a few things to consider after buying term insurance.
Check the policy document
Following the purchase of the policy, there is a free-look period of 15-30 days, during which you can return your policy if you find something you do not agree with.
As a policyholder, after you purchase the plan and receive the policy document, take time to read it. If you come up with any doubts or notice any discrepancies in the same, you can bring them up with your policy provider and get the details ironed out. Thus, one of the first things to do after buying term insurance is to go through the policy document.
Notify the nominees
Once your policy purchase is complete and all the policy details are sorted, the next thing to do after buying term insurance should be to inform the nominee about the policy purchase and ensure they understand what it entails. If there are multiple nominees, everyone should be aware of the process and the facts.
If you haven’t already named a nominee (or nominees) in your policy, get the nomination process started. It can be done anytime. You can also change your nominee when the policy is in force. During the nomination process, ensure you provide the correct details of the person you are nominating. Also, ensure that the nominee knows about the policy.
Create a premium payment setup
Something to consider after buying term insurance is that if you do not pay your premiums on time. If you do not pay your premiums, you risk a policy lapse. However, in the hustle and bustle of your regular routines, the term life insurance premium payment date may pass you by, and you may not notice. To avoid such a situation, you can set reminders for the same. Alternatively, you can also set up auto-debit systems that directly pay your premium from your bank account to your insurance provider.
Whether the premiums be annual or monthly, you ought to pay them on time without letting the amount accrue and burn a hole in your pocket. You can use a term insurance premium calculator before buying your term plan. The premium estimates allow you to determine whether you can afford the premiums for the chosen policy coverage and payment frequency or whether you need to make any changes to the same.
Know the claims process
Once you buy term insurance, you should be well versed with the procedure to claim the benefits for the same. The procedure is fairly similar across insurance providers. Your nominee should also know how to claim the policy benefits if the need should arise.
Keep the policy document safe
The policy document is arguably the most important document for you as well as your nominee when it comes to term insurance. Once you receive the policy document and read through it, you must keep it safe. File all the papers and keep them in order. If possible, label the file for your nominee to access these papers quickly in times of need.
Create a file of your health record
One of the things to do after buying term insurance that most people neglect is maintaining your health records. Your nominee may require accessing your health records when initiating a claim. Hence, keep a record of your health (all your medical bills, prescriptions, and other relevant documents). You can also make copies of the same and attach them to the policy-documents file. Let your nominee know how to access the file. Such precautions are necessary if you have taken a critical illness rider with your term plan.
Maintain your health
Remember that buying term insurance is not the one-stop solution to your well-being. It is only a financial safety net that can protect your family in unfortunate situations. Thus, even after you buy term insurance, take care of your health if you haven’t started yet. Avoid smoking, alcohol, and a sedentary lifestyle. Eat healthy and stay active.
These were some of the most important things to consider after buying term insurance. Once you get term insurance, focus on paying the premiums on time and keeping yourself and your family healthy. To ensure the premiums are within your budget, use a term insurance premium calculator before buying the policy.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.

Things To Do After Buying Term Insurance
Buying term life insurance is one of the significant steps you may take to ensure the financial well-being of your family, in your absence. But does your responsibility to protect your family end there? Making the purchase of life insurance policy may seem to be the end of the process of getting a life cover, but it is also the first step in ensuring that your plan covers you well and your family gets the financial protection you want for them.
Any person associated with the policy or benefiting from it should be aware of all the things to do after buying term insurance. After you purchase a term plan, don’t assume that all you will have to do now, is making the payment of premiums. You should ensure that you get the required benefits out of the policy.
Steps After Getting a Term Plan
Here are a few things to consider after buying term insurance.
 Check the policy document
Following the purchase of the policy, there is a free-look period of 15 days, and 30 days (in case of electronic policies and policies sourced through distance mode), during which you can place a request for cancellation of your policy if you find something you do not agree with.
As a policyholder, after you purchase the plan and receive the policy document, take time to read it. If you come up with any doubts or notice any discrepancies in the same, you can bring them up with your policy provider and get the errors ironed out. Thus, one of the first things to do after buying term insurance is to go through the policy document.
 Notify the nominees
Once your policy purchase is complete and all the policy details are sorted, the next thing to do after buying term insurance should be to inform the nominee about the policy purchase and ensure they understand what it entails. If there are multiple nominees, everyone should be aware of the process and the facts.
If you haven’t already named a nominee (or nominees) in your policy, get the nomination process started. It can be done anytime. You can also change your nominee when the policy is in force. During the nomination process, ensure you provide the correct details of the person you are nominating.
 Create a premium payment setup
Something to consider after buying term insurance is that if you do not pay your premiums on time, you risk a policy lapse. However, in the hustle and bustle of your regular routines, the term life insurance premium payment date may pass you by, and you may not notice. To avoid such a situation, you can set reminders for the same. Alternatively, you can also register for auto-debit facility, if provided by the Insurer, that directly pay your premium from your bank account to your insurance provider.
You can use a term insurance premium calculator before buying your term plan. The premium estimates allow you to determine whether you can afford the premiums for the chosen policy coverage and payment frequency or whether you need to make any changes to the same.
 Know the claims process
Once you buy term insurance, you should be well versed with the procedure to claim the benefits for the same. The procedure is fairly similar across all insurance providers. Your nominee should also know how to claim the policy benefits if the need should arise.
 Keep the policy document safe
The policy document is an important document for you as well as for your nominee when it comes to term insurance. Once you receive the policy document and read through it, you must keep it safe. You may file all the papers and keep them in order. If possible, label the file for your nominee to access these papers quickly in times of need.
 Create a file of your health record
One of the things to do after buying term insurance that people may neglect is maintaining health records. Your nominee may require accessing your health records when initiating a claim. Hence, keep a record of your health (all your medical bills, prescriptions, and other relevant documents). You can also make copies of the same and attach them to the policy-documents file. Let your nominee know how to access the file.
 Maintain your health
Remember that buying term insurance is not the one-stop solution to your well-being. It is only a financial safety net that can protect your family in unfortunate situations. Thus, even after you buy term insurance, take care of your health if you haven’t started yet. You may consider avoiding smoking, alcohol, and a sedentary lifestyle. Eat healthy and stay active.
These were some of the important things to consider after buying term insurance. Once you get term insurance, focus on paying the premiums on time and keeping yourself and your family healthy. To ensure the premiums are within your budget, use a term insurance premium calculator before buying the policy.
